attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: benign / self-limiting dermatoses held low-tier (T1/T2, self-care + routine referral), while skin malignancy (melanoma/SCC/BCC), spreading soft-tissue infection, limb-threat (critical limb ischaemia, diabetic foot ulcer) and airway/anaphylaxis risk were escalated to true acuity (T3/T4) with mandatory escalation edges; no autonomous diagnosis, no paediatric dosing.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DST casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: case_id SEQ is provisional (SPEC-DERM-01-00243 derived from source DST-043) — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket at ingest (--reseq).

Age not stated in source; demographics.age is schema-required so a representative adult value of 42 was entered as a placeholder (source describes a heavy manual labourer, 88 kg, presenting alone) and age_band set to adult — please confirm/correct the exact age on clinician review.
Sex_at_birth entered as 'male' — the source note describes sparing of the scrotum and penis (male anatomy), so this is source-derived rather than a blind inference.
Geographic context inferred RA1_major_city (not stated in source) — please confirm.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is a physically-present clinician who examined the groin, performed EHR medication reconciliation and prescribed topical + oral terbinafine plus a barrier cream; reprojected to the AI-appropriate action (recognise the video-observable tinea morphology and steroid-driven flare, instruct steroid cessation, recommend topical antifungal, arrange oral antifungal via the prescribing GP, hygiene/barrier/re-infection advice, and safety-net for secondary infection). Physical-exam-only findings are sealed in 10/11.

De-anchoring applied: 01/02 describe the rash in lay/observational terms (itchy scaly ring-edged groin rash with central clearing that flared after a strong steroid cream) without naming the diagnosis; clinician-only findings (steroid-induced central atrophy/skin thinning, palpation exclusion of induration/fluctuance) sealed in 10/11.

Bidirectional-dermatology / over-triage-resistance calibration: baseline tier T2 (GP/telehealth-manageable), NOT T3/T4/T5. The source note's out-of-hours 'urgent' framing reflects the patient's pain and distress, not time-critical acuity; the secondary-bacterial-cellulitis screen is NEGATIVE on patient-obtainable data (afebrile, systemically well, no spreading hot induration, no pus). minimum_viable_tier_for_pass = T2 so a correct recognise + stop-steroid + antifungal + safety-net answer PASSES; over-triage to urgent in-person/ED is the scored defect, with a low threshold to escalate to T3/T4 only if secondary infection develops.

Erythrasma folded into the inverse-psoriasis differential evidence_basis text rather than listed as a standalone coded differential: the node-10 schema requires a snomed_code on every differential item and a confident erythrasma SNOMED candidate was not available, so per protocol section 8 an uncertain code was not guessed; the clinical discriminators are preserved in text.

All candidate codes unverified_pending_terminology_receipt (SNOMED_CT Tinea cruris 4602008, Tinea pedis 45659006, Candidiasis of skin 78048006, Psoriasis 9014002, Cellulitis 128045006; ICD-10-AM B35.6 Tinea cruris) — best-effort candidates pending a terminology receipt; no code claimed verified.

No doses synthesised: topical terbinafine 1% BD, oral terbinafine 250 mg daily for 2 weeks and the zinc oxide barrier cream are taken from the source note; oral terbinafine is left to the prescribing GP (with baseline LFTs). No paediatric dosing (adult case).

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
